rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA ZEVALLOS VS. MARILYN CHAVEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltf'S Verified First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 24, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS EXCEL REALTY & MORTGAGE INC, JOSEPH CHAVEZ, SHAWNACY NAUROTH Motion to Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Verified First Amended Complaint. Motion to strike denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA ZEVALLOS VS. MARILYN CHAVEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 24, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS EXCEL REALTY & MORTGAGE INC, JOSEPH CHAVEZ, and, SHAWNACY NAUROTH DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer overruled as to 8th, 14th, and 15th causes of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 10th through 13th causes of action. As to the 10th and 11th causes of action, plaintiff must allege that defendants had actual knowledge of the contracts. As to the 12th cause of action, plaintiff must plead facts that support a legally recognized duty imposed on defendants. The 13th cause of action must be supported by specific unfair or unlawful practices. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TRI-WEST, LTD EMPLOYEE HEALTH BENEFIT PLAN VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 24, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST. MARY'S MEDICAL, Motion To Transfer. Is denied. Defendant fails to meet its burden to show that the action is improper in San Francisco. Defendant fails to establish by admissible evidence that the convenience of witnesses or the ends of justice would be promoted by transferring the case to Los Angeles. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TRI-WEST, LTD EMPLOYEE HEALTH BENEFIT PLAN VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 24, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST. MARY'S MEDICAL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend for plaintiff to plead ultimate facts. Court cannot consider extrinsic evidence on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN SEEWER VS. WELLS FARGO INVESTMENTS, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 24, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS WELLS FARGO INVESTMENTS, LLC, PATRICK BIGGS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 1st and 2nd causes of action as to Briggs. A supervisor cannot be individually liable on those claims as a matter of law. Demurrer overruled as to Wells Fargo Investments, 10 days leave to answer. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads that he was constructively terminated because of his sexual orientation. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN SEEWER VS. WELLS FARGO INVESTMENTS, LLC et al

rulings.Case Title: MILLENIUM PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. VS. TRISHA HOLLERAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction (Transferred From Dept. 301)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 24, 2010, line 11, Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction (Transferred From Dept. 301). Motion granted. Plaintiff is likely to prevail and has demonstrated at a minimum, threatened misappropriation by defendant's words and conduct. Plaintiff demonstrates great injury if the injunction is denied. Defendant will not suffer any injury if the injunction is granted. The court adopts plaintiff's supplemental proposed order. =(302/CWW)
